title: cnicus horridulus
intro: Cnicus horridulus, also known as Rough-hair knapweed, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cnicus horridulus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be applied to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
